title: [FIRRTL] IMCP Issue with Register Reset
body:
IMCP is choosing to optimize a register with an invalidated reset to the reset value. This is causing a divergence with the Scala FIRRTL Compiler (SFC).

Consider:

```scala
circuit Foo :
  module Foo :
    input clock: Clock
    output b: UInt<1>

    inst bar of Bar
    bar.clock <= clock
    bar.a is invalid
    b <= bar.b
    
  module Bar :
    input clock : Clock
    input a: UInt<1>
    output b: UInt<1>

    reg r : UInt<1>, clock with: (reset => (a, UInt<1>(1)))
    r is invalid

    b <= r
```

CIRCT produces:

```verilog
module Foo(	// Foo.fir:2:10
  input  clock,
  output b);

  Bar bar ();	// Foo.fir:6:5
  assign b = 1'h1;	// Foo.fir:2:10, :6:5
endmodule
```

SFC produces:
```verilog
module Foo(
  input   clock,
  output  b
);
  assign b = 1'h0;
endmodule
```
